calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petitioner Juul Labs, Inc'S Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Dec-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eli Lazarus, Esq. 415-393-8200 of Gibson Dunn for Petitioner JUUL LABS, INC.
Brian Johnsrud, Esq. 650-600-5310 of Curley Hurtgen &Johnsrud LLP for Claimant/Petitioner Juul Labs.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JUUL LABS, INC.'s Petition To Compel Arbitration.

uul Labs, Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is granted. Respondent Siddharth Breja and Juul contracted to arbitrate "any claim, controversy or legal dispute." (Johnsrud Dec. Ex. B.)

To avoid arbitration, Breja had to show both procedural and substantive unconscionability. (See Baltazar v. Forever 21 (2016) 62 Cal.4th 1237, 1243.) As to the former, Breja declares that he had no chance to negotiate employment terms. However, Breja pled in federal court that he negotiated terms with Juul and it "agreed to" his "proposed changes." (Dhillon Dec. Ex. A 10:26-28.) As to the latter, the parties applied CCP 1280 et seq. to their arbitration and thus agreed to a neutral arbitrator, discovery and a written award. (Id. at 1281.6, 1283.4, 1283.05.)

CCP 1281.2(c) does not apply as Breja is not litigating with a third party. Arbitration is compelled because the Federal Arbitration Act governs the parties' agreement. (Rodriguez v. American Technologies, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 1110, 1115, 1117-22.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
